title: 'Code' style for Messages and Comments
excerpt:

When I add HTML to messages/comments, it becomes a part of real HTML and then is being uglifyed. I want a ‘code’ style for the editor, in which no code will be formatted/processed, and w…

posts.author: Aleksei (Aleksei)
posts.cookedHtml:
What is your current pain point?

When I add HTML to messages/comments, it becomes a part of real HTML and then is being uglifyed. I want a ‘code’ style for the editor, in which no code will be formatted/processed, and will be styled with a monospace font.

What do you have in mind to solve it?

It’s a simple feature that can be added to the message text editor. Exactly as it is done here in the community text editor.

It is extremely strange that there’s still no such a feature.

posts.author: jethompson (Jeremy Thompson)
posts.cookedHtml:
Not sure if this is what you were looking for, but it’s what I was looking for. And at first I didn’t think they had it, since it’s kind of tucked away:

I even made a tongue-in-cheek comment above about it, which is what made me take another glance (and which I’ve now - happily - deleted. ).

posts.author: daveken (Dave K)
posts.cookedHtml: I see that in the rich text editor when creating a pulse. But what about replies?

posts.author: IsaacNewton (C)
posts.cookedHtml:
any way of doing this in replies?

would be super helpful, if we could even use backticks for inline code or

triple backticks for code blocks

posts.author: IsaacNewton (C)
posts.cookedHtml: Ha! they worked here and changed the comments into code… would be nice if that worked within Monday too

posts.author: flaviusstef (Flavius Stef)
posts.cookedHtml:
I second that. The .dev part of monday.dev needs to get some love. The community portal has it much better

  1. Support for markdown syntax `
  2. Support for markdown syntax ```
  3. Full support for them in description, updates and update replies
  4. Option to choose between wrapping and not wrapping code which has multiple lines. If I paste a bunch of loglines in a ticket’s updates, it wraps and creates a 2000px wall of text, and it’s not clear where one line ends and another one starts
